# @cdlab/utils usage guide for AI agents

`@cdlab/utils` is a build-only ESM library of shared browser + Node primitives:
clipboard copy, file download, byte formatting, IndexedDB key-value storage,
timestamped logging, precise decimal arithmetic (`np`), and Argon2id password
hashing. Seven independent modules re-exported from one barrel, consumed across
the monorepo as a `workspace:*` dependency. No server, no routes, no env vars, no
Cloudflare bindings, no network calls.

Primary docs:

- README: ./README.md
- Design spec (authoritative): ./DESIGN.md
- Barrel entry: ./src/index.ts

Part of the `@cdlab/projects-monorepo`; run all commands from the repo root with
`pnpm --filter @cdlab/utils <script>`.

## What it is

- **Build-only library** — `tsdown` bundles `src/index.ts` → `dist/index.mjs` +
  `dist/index.d.mts`. Consumers import from the package root ONLY (no deep subpath
  exports besides `./package.json`).
- **`dist/` is prebuilt and committed**; consumers resolve `dist/`, not `src/`.
- **Runtime split**: universal (Node + browser) = `format`, `logger`, `np`,
  `password`; browser-only (need DOM / `window` / `indexedDB`) = `clipboard`,
  `download`, `idb-store`.
- Build target `ES2018` (tsdown); TS compile target `ES6` via
  `@cdlab/tsconfig/utils.json`.

## Modules & key files

| Module | File | Exports |
| --- | --- | --- |
| clipboard | src/clipboard.ts | `copyToClipboard(message, opts?)` |
| download | src/download.ts | `downloadFile({ data, filename })`, `downloadFilesAsZip(files, prefix)` |
| format | src/format.ts | `formatBytes({ bytes, decimals? })`, `formatFileSize` (deprecated) |
| idb-store | src/idb-store.ts | `createIDBStore<T>(dbName, storeName?, version?)`, `deleteIDBDatabase(dbName)` |
| logger | src/logger.ts | `logger` (`log`/`info`/`warn`/`error`/`debug`) |
| np | src/np/index.ts | `plus`/`minus`/`times`/`divide`/`round`/`strip`/`digitLength`/`float2Fixed`/`enableBoundaryChecking`/`createCalculator`/`NumberCalculator` |
| password | src/password.ts | `hashPasswordFn(password, salt?)`, `verifyPasswordFn(storedHash, attempt)` |

## Behavior notes (read before editing)

- `copyToClipboard` — tries `navigator.clipboard.writeText`, falls back to
  `execCommand('copy')` when `fallbackToLegacy` (default true). Returns a boolean,
  NEVER throws. iOS Safari uses `Range`/`Selection`, not `.select()`.
- `downloadFile` — revokes the object URL ONLY when it created it from a Blob; if
  the caller passes a URL string, the caller owns revoke.
- `downloadFilesAsZip` — `jszip` is `await import('jszip')` (dynamic) so it stays
  out of consumers' main bundles. ZIP name = `{prefix}_yyyyMMdd_HHmmss.zip`
  (`date-fns`).
- `np` — ops accept numbers OR numeric strings, are variadic; `plus`/`minus` are
  built on `times`. `enableBoundaryChecking` toggles a MODULE-GLOBAL mutable flag;
  boundary breaches only `console.warn`, never throw.
- `password` — Argon2id (`@noble/hashes`, `t:3 m:1280 p:4 dkLen:32`); stored as
  `saltHex:hashHex`. `verify` uses plain `===` (NOT constant-time) and THROWS on a
  malformed hash. `async` API but synchronous hashing.
- `logger` — timestamps are `toLocaleString()` (local tz, locale-dependent), not
  ISO/UTC. Do not machine-parse them.

## Config / bindings

- None. No env vars, no secrets, no Cloudflare bindings, no network. Fully
  self-contained.
- Runtime deps (all `catalog:prod`): `@noble/hashes` (password), `date-fns`
  (download timestamp), `jszip` (download, lazy).
- Build: `tsdown.config.ts` (single entry, esm, dts, ES2018, clean unless
  `--watch`). Test: `vitest.config.ts` (`@`→`src`, `test/**/*.test.ts`).

## Gotchas for agents

- REBUILD after editing source: consumers resolve committed `dist/index.mjs`, so
  source edits are invisible until `pnpm --filter @cdlab/utils build` (or `dev`).
- The barrel `export * from './np'` does NOT re-export np's `default` (`NP`) —
  `export *` drops defaults. Import named functions from the package root; `NP`
  is submodule-only.
- Import from the package root, not deep paths — only `.` and `./package.json` are
  exported.
- Browser-only modules (`clipboard`, `download`, `idb-store`) throw in Node — do
  not call them on a Node/Worker code path.
- No tests exist for `download` or `idb-store` (DOM-bound); don't assume coverage.
- Lint/format is Biome (single quotes, no semicolons, 2-space) — NOT ESLint (the
  `eslint-disable` comments in `logger.ts` are legacy, harmless).

## Common commands

```bash
pnpm --filter @cdlab/utils build       # tsdown -> dist/
pnpm --filter @cdlab/utils dev         # tsdown --watch
pnpm --filter @cdlab/utils test        # vitest --run
pnpm --filter @cdlab/utils test:watch  # vitest watch
pnpm --filter @cdlab/utils typecheck   # tsc --noEmit
```
